conjecture_title: Conjecture on Sufficiency of Conditions E, R, T for Generic Tuples
summary: Ankit Bisain (arXiv:2209.13811, September 2022; published BLMS 2024) resolves the conjecture by classifying dope matrices when the entries of Λ are algebraically independent (i.e., generic), establishing that Conditions E, R, T are necessary and sufficient for an m×(n+1) matrix to be the multiplicity matrix of a polynomial with respect to a generic tuple. The paper also provides asymptotic upper and lower bounds on the total number of m×(n+1) dope matrices.

since_posted.title: Generic Classification and Asymptotic Enumeration of Dope Matrices
since_posted.venue: Bulletin of the London Mathematical Society
since_posted.claim: Resolves the conjecture by classifying dope matrices when Λ is algebraically independent (generic), proving that Conditions E, R, T are necessary and sufficient for an m×(n+1) matrix to be the multiplicity matrix of some polynomial P with respect to Λ.

search_queries: Alon Kravitz O'Bryant "dope matrices" "multiplicity matrix" conjecture 2205.09302
"dope matrix" "multiplicity matrix" polynomial generic tuple conditions sufficient 2022 2023 2024
notes: Bisain's paper (arXiv:2209.13811) was submitted to arXiv in September 2022, shortly after the source paper appeared in May 2022, and was published in the Bulletin of the London Mathematical Society in 2024 (doi:10.1112/blms.12924). The arXiv abstract page confirms it 'resolves a conjecture of Alon, Kravitz, and O'Bryant' by classifying dope matrices when Λ is algebraically independent; a secondary search result explicitly states that conditions E, R, T are necessary and sufficient for generic tuples, matching the conjecture exactly. The BLMS journal page returned HTTP 402 (paywall) and the PDF was binary-encoded, so the conclusion relies on the arXiv abstract and search-result descriptions.
